Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

## registry (1 records, updated just now)

### Conventions
- [mx-91d2b5] LEVERET_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts, warren-fdd9) is the first 'conversation'-tagged built-in and the first with runtime='pi-chat' — readRuntimeId forwards frontmatter.runtime as a free string so no KNOWN_RUNTIME_IDS change is needed. It ships its single pi extension via the sections.pi_extensions JSONL {name,body} line (seeded to .pi/extensions/propose_intent.ts by buildSeedFiles). The propose_intent tool is a pure proposal carrier: execute() returns the field-scoped intent patch ({goal,non_goals,constraints,success_criteria}) on result.details and writes nothing — warren correlates it host-side via tool_execution_end toolCallId into intent_edited(actor=leveret). The def grants read-leaning tools only (read/grep/find/ls/bash, NO edit/write) so the send-off PR is plot-state-only by construction.

## runs (4 records, updated just now)

### Conventions
- [mx-b41dbd] warren-c770 / LEVERET.md §0.4: the pi-chat runtime suppresses the per-turn agent_end terminal envelope, so a conversation run stays non-terminal across turns. Three warren-side lifetime guards gained an explicit conversation skip: (1) heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts) — 'if (run.mode==="conversation") continue' before computeIdleMs so an armed WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS can't force-fail idle conversations; (2) reap workspace-destroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts) — extends the interactive exemption to conversation, emitting reap.workspace_destroy_skipped reason 'conversation_run'; (3) bootBridges crash-recovery (src/server/bridges.ts) — on a pre-probe 404 for a conversation run, skip the bridge with reason 'conversation_burrow_lost' WITHOUT calling reconcileLostBurrowRun, leaving the row non-terminal so re-wake (warren-6ccf) replays the transcript. Pause detector (pause.ts guards mode!=='batch') and workspace GC (state-keyed) already exempt conversation for free. Also added 'conversation' to RUN_MODES in src/db/schema/columns.ts.

### Patterns
- [mx-ab5467] **conversation-rewake-seam-primitive**: Leveret build-phase-2 lifetime/re-wake primitives (conversation-idle.ts, conversation-rewake.ts) are landed as pure orchestration + seam interfaces (reader/rotator/spawner) BEFORE the warren-0b91 conversations/messages tables exist. rewakeConversation() takes a ConversationRewakeReader + ConversationAnchorRotator seam and an injectable RewakeSpawner (defaults to spawnRun), so unit tests stay disk/table-free; production repo-backed impls slot in at the HTTP boundary (warren-af15). Re-wake refuses non-active conversations and non-terminal anchoring runs, replays the full DB transcript verbatim via buildRewakePrompt into a fresh mode:'conversation' spawnRun, rotates anchoring_run_id, then emits conversation.rewake_replayed. No pi --session resume / old burrow dependency.

### Known Failures
- [mx-e79c70] warren-ce65 (conversation stream-bridge: persist turns to messages + parse propose_intent from tool_execution_end + write intent_edited(actor=agent:leveret) host-side) is hard-blocked by two unmet dependencies as of 2026-06-06. (2) The ../plot ACL widening (EVENT_ACL.intent_edited -> ['user','agent']) NOT shipped: published @os-eco/plot-cli@0.4.0 still pins intent_edited:['user'] (asserted in its own acl.test.ts) and assertCanEmit rejects agents ('agents must surface a question_posed event instead of mutating intent'). warren's facade also still lists intent_edited in HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES (src/plot-client/types.ts). An agent:leveret intent_edited write throws at both facade and lib runtime. The plot repo is not co-mounted (no ../plot; origin is warren only) so the ACL cannot be widened from this run.

cd /workspace && cat > /tmp/scrub.py <<'PY'
import re, sys

files = [
"src/server/bridges.conversation.test.ts",
"src/server/main/detector-wiring.ts",
"src/server/handlers/plots/workbench.ts",
"src/server/handlers/conversations.ts",
"src/db/schema/postgres.ts",
"src/db/schema/columns.ts",
"src/db/schema/sqlite.ts",
"src/db/repos/messages.ts",
"src/db/repos/conversations.ts",
"src/runs/conversation-merge-poller.ts",
"src/runs/conversation-rewake.ts",
"src/runs/spawn/types.ts",
"src/runs/conversation-idle.test.ts",
"src/runs/conversation-rewake.test.ts",
"src/runs/watchdog.ts",
"src/runs/conversation-idle.ts",
"src/warren-config/schema.ts",
"src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts",
"src/ui/src/api/types.ts",
"src/ui/src/pages/conversation-detail/dispatch-plan-dialog.tsx",
"src/ui/src/pages/plot-detail/interactive-panel.tsx",
"src/ui/src/pages/Leveret.tsx",
"src/ui/src/pages/ConversationDetail.tsx",
"scripts/acceptance/scenarios/33-leveret-conversation-loop.ts",
"CLAUDE.md",
]

# matches LEVERET.md §x.x optionally chained with / §y.y
cite = re.compile(r'LEVERET\.md\s*§[0-9.A-Z]+(?:\s*/\s*§[0-9.A-Z]+)*')

for f in files:
    with open(f) as fh:
        orig = fh.read()
    out = []
    for line in orig.split('\n'):
        if 'LEVERET.md' not in line:
            out.append(line); continue
        l = cite.sub('\x00', line)
        # now tidy around the \x00 placeholder
        # case: "(warren-xxx / \x00)" -> remove " / \x00"
        l = re.sub(r'\s*/\s*\x00', '', l)
        # case: "(\x00 / warren-xxx)" -> remove "\x00 / "
        l = re.sub(r'\x00\s*/\s*', '', l)
        # case: standalone "(\x00)" -> remove " (\x00)"
        l = re.sub(r'\s*\(\x00\)', '', l)
        # leftover bare placeholder with surrounding parens/commas
        l = re.sub(r',?\s*\x00', '', l)
        out.append(l)
    new = '\n'.join(out)
    if new != orig:
        with open(f,'w') as fh:
            fh.write(new)
        print("updated", f)
PY
python3 /tmp/scrub.py

cd /workspace && cat > /tmp/scrub2.py <<'PY'
import re
files = [
"src/plots/projection-sink.ts",
"src/db/schema/postgres.ts",
"src/db/schema/columns.ts",
"src/db/schema/sqlite.ts",
"src/db/repos/plots.ts",
"src/plot-client/projection.ts",
]
cite = re.compile(r'LEVERET\s*§[0-9.A-Z]+(?:\s*/\s*§[0-9.A-Z]+)*')
for f in files:
    orig=open(f).read()
    out=[]
    for line in orig.split('\n'):
        if 'LEVERET' not in line:
            out.append(line); continue
        l=cite.sub('\x00',line)
        l=re.sub(r'\s*/\s*\x00','',l)
        l=re.sub(r'\x00\s*/\s*','',l)
        l=re.sub(r'\s*\(\x00\)','',l)
        l=re.sub(r',?\s*\x00','',l)
        out.append(l)
    new='\n'.join(out)
    if new!=orig:
        open(f,'w').write(new); print("updated",f)
PY
python3 /tmp/scrub2.py
